gui.recover.about.title: About
gui.recover.about.tagline: Free recovery tool for Nyx Backup.
gui.recover.about.blurb: Restores from any Nyx Backup endpoint without requiring the main app, a license, or a running service.  Credentials and master keys live in memory only - nothing is persisted to disk except restore checkpoints and convenience preferences (theme, recent endpoints).
gui.recover.about.legal: Copyright (c) 2026 Nyx Software, LLC.  Apache-2.0 licensed - free to use, audit, modify, fork, and redistribute.  The on-disk format that this tool reads is published in the Nyx Backup data-format specification.
gui.recover.unlock.title: Unlock
gui.recover.unlock.desc: Paste the 64-character hexadecimal master key from your other machine (or a KEY=<hex> file exported with nyx_bkp_cli export-key).
gui.recover.unlock.placeholder: KEY=abcd1234... (64 hex chars, or 68 with the KEY= prefix)
gui.recover.unlock.mode_b_note: Mode B (recovery passphrase + machine ID) lands in a future build.
gui.recover.unlock.file_picked: File picker selected {path}.  Open the file in a text editor and paste its contents into the field below.
gui.recover.unlock.err_empty: Paste your master key or load it from a file
gui.recover.settings.title: Settings
gui.recover.settings.bandwidth_label: Download bandwidth (Mbps - leave blank for unlimited)
gui.recover.settings.bandwidth_desc: Applied via RateLimitedBackend at restore time; browsing is uncapped.
gui.recover.settings.language_desc: 'Auto' follows your OS language and falls back to English if it isn't one of the 24 supported.  Pick a specific language to override - useful if your OS is set to a language not in the list.  Restart the Recovery Tool for the change to take full effect.
gui.recover.settings.log_desc: Logs at ~/.local/share/nyxbackup-recover/logs/recovery.log (or platform equivalent).  Restart for the change to apply.
gui.recover.browse.err_no_files: Select at least one file or folder
gui.recover.browse.err_no_space: Not enough free space at destination
gui.recover.browse.select_snapshot: Select a snapshot to see its file tree.
gui.recover.browse.crossplatform_note: Cross-platform paths (e.g. C:\Users\... on Linux) are auto-remapped under the chosen folder.
gui.recover.browse.no_files_hint: No files selected - tick at least one to restore
gui.recover.connect.title: Connect to a backup endpoint
gui.recover.connect.paste_token: Or paste an existing refresh token
gui.recover.connect.ph_required_smb: required when the share needs auth
gui.recover.connect.ph_gcs_json: Paste the contents of your service account JSON key file
gui.recover.connect.ph_refresh_token: Paste the refresh token exported from the source machine
gui.recover.connect.ph_label: A friendly name for this endpoint
gui.recover.connect.master_key_blurb: The 32-byte master key (64 hex characters) that encrypts the backup set on the source machine. This key is the only thing needed to decrypt your data - the storage credentials above just let us reach the bytes. If the source machine is still around, export the key with nyx_bkp_cli export-key or copy it from Settings > Recovery on that machine. If the source machine is lost, you'll need the copy you saved when you first set up the backup - this is why we recommended keeping it somewhere safe (password manager, printed and locked away, etc.).
gui.recover.connect.file_hint: Lines starting with # and blank lines are ignored when loading from a file.
gui.recover.connect.field_test: Test connection (click the button - must show ✓)
gui.recover.connect.mtls_hint_sftp: Leave blank for password auth. When set, the password field above is treated as the key passphrase (leave it blank if the key is unencrypted).
gui.recover.connect.mtls_hint_webdav: Only needed if your WebDAV server requires a TLS client certificate. Provide the certificate and its private key together in one PEM file.
err.not_connected: Not connected yet. Complete the Connect step first.
err.not_unlocked: The backup is locked. Enter your recovery key to unlock it first.
err.badkey: The recovery key is not valid. Paste the full key exactly as it was shown when the backup was created.
err.decrypt: Could not decrypt the backup. The recovery key may be incorrect, or the data may be in an unsupported format.
err.disk: There is not enough free disk space at the restore destination.
err.permission: Access was denied. Check that your credentials or keys have permission to read this data.
err.auth: Sign-in failed. Check your access key, secret, username, or password.
err.not_found: The storage location or backup data could not be found. Double-check the address, bucket, or path.
err.no_backups: No backups were found at this location. Double-check the address, bucket, or folder path.
err.restore_missing: Some of the backup's data could not be found in storage. The backup may be incomplete, or the wrong location was selected.
err.timeout: The storage server did not respond in time. Check your connection and try again.
err.network: Could not reach the storage server. Check the address and your internet connection.
err.badinput: The selected backup or snapshot could not be identified. Try reloading the snapshot list.
err.connect_generic: Could not connect to the storage location. Check the address and credentials.
err.unlock_generic: Could not unlock the backup with this recovery key.
err.list_generic: Could not read the backups at this location.
err.restore_generic: The restore could not be completed.
err.oauth_generic: Sign-in did not complete. Please try again.
err.readfile_generic: Could not read the selected file.
err.generic: Something went wrong. See the log for details.
gui.recover.browse.err_restore_failed: The restore could not be completed.
gui.recover.settings.sparse_desc: Punch all-zero regions as filesystem holes so restored VM disk images and pre-allocated files keep a small footprint. The restored data is identical either way. Turn off to write every byte (maximum compatibility).
gui.recover.connect.s3_region_ph: us-east-1 (leave blank unless your provider requires a specific region)
err.archive: This backup is in cold storage (Glacier / Archive tier). The data must be retrieved (thawed) before it can be restored - this can take several hours and may incur provider fees. Start the retrieval with your storage provider, then try again.
gui.recover.connect.subtitle: The Recovery Tool reads from any Nyx Backup endpoint without requiring the main app or a license. Credentials and the master key live in memory only - nothing is persisted to disk.
gui.recover.connect.err_endpoint_require: Endpoint URL is required for S3-compatible backends (e.g. https://s3.wasabisys.com)
gui.recover.connect.url_label_gdrive: Google Drive folder URL or folder ID
gui.recover.browse.no_dest_default: Could not determine a default restore folder - choose Custom and pick one
gui.filetree.hint: ↑↓ navigate · ▶/▼ or click 📁 to expand · checkbox to select · Space toggle · right-click for options
gui.recover.connect.manual_summary: No browser on this machine? (manual sign-in)
gui.recover.connect.manual_gdrive_hint: Enter your Drive folder URL/ID in the field above first.
gui.recover.connect.manual_step1: 1. Open this link in any browser (phone, another PC) and approve:
gui.recover.connect.manual_step2: 2. Your browser then lands on a "can't reach localhost" page - copy that page's full address (it contains ?code=...) and paste it here:
gui.recover.connect.paste_token_hint: On the source machine, inspect the keyring entry named nyxbackup.ep-NNNN.secret (Windows Credential Manager / macOS Keychain / libsecret).
gui.recover.browse.no_snapshots: No snapshots reachable with this key.
